mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 45%) over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% month-over-month signals supply-side capacity utilization tightening, triggering short-term profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a static or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) for 60-day forward returns across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% indicates economic stagnation that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment signals inefficient capacity utilization and structural labor market weakness, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns (accuracy < 40%) in walk-forward tests.
